title: Cynthia L. Shearn | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP

With nearly 30 years of dedicated immigration law experience, Cindy understands the positive impact listening can have on both clients and colleagues alike. Using this skill, she strikes the delicate balance of helping clients achieve their business goals while successfully creating and organizing their effective immigration programs, representing multinational corporate clients in a range of industries, including management and technology consulting, logistics and supply chain, transportation, pharmaceuticals and biotechnology.
Since being appointed Managing Partner of Fragomen’s Midwest practice at the beginning of 2023, Cindy has also taken on a managerial role in overseeing both the region’s practice and client relationships—successfully listening to and advocating for her colleagues and employees. In each of these capacities—both as an immigration lawyer and as a people manager—Cindy serves as a trusted advisor and true partner by recognizing that it is her responsibility to understand their needs and provide the space to communicate them.
Cindy has always been innately focused on helping others—in fact, this focus is what propelled her into a career in immigration law. As an international relations major in college, she knew she was interested in human rights and global issues. During her first year of law school, she was granted an opportunity to blend these passions by helping an individual and her family with an asylum application. She filed the application, attended their interviews and eventually celebrated when they were granted asylum—realizing she found her calling to pursue a career an immigration law.
Passionate about inclusivity, Cindy previously served as Chair and currently serves as Co-Chair of Fragomen’s U.S./Canada DEI Group, helping to lead its expansion across the entire region. She is an active Fragomen and community volunteer, including through her pro bono work conducting Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) and naturalization clinics and providing services to immigrant families at the detention center in Dilley, Texas. She also is a founding member of Nexus49 , a Canada-U.S. business council in Chicago, and serves as a board member of the British-American Business Council’s Chicago Chapter.

- American University, Washington College of Law, J.D., 1996
- Boston University, B.A., 1991
Bar Admissions: Illinois
Practice Limited to Federal Immigration & Nationality Law
Listed in Chambers USA, 2024 to 2025
Listed in The American Lawyers’ Midwest Trailblazers, 2022
Listed among Crain’s 2026 Notable Women in Law
Listed among Crain’s 2020 Notable Women Executives Over 50
Phi Beta Kappa, 1991
Harold C. Case Scholar, 1991

### Professional accomplishments
Founding Member, Nexus49
Member, Board of Directors for the British-American Business Council, Chicago
Member, American Immigration Lawyers Association, Chicago Chapter
Member, Chicago Bar Association
Manages high volume U.S. immigration practice for global consulting, technology and outsourcing company to facilitate the transfer of professional employees from around the world to the United States.

### Publications and lectures
Speaker, “Humanitarian Pathways & Workforce Strategy: Adapting to Evolving U.S. Immigration Policy.” Fragomen , Webinar, 5 August 2025.
Speaker, “Navigating the Path to U.S. Citizenship: What You Need to Know.” Fragomen , Webinar, 16 July 2025.
Speaker, “Navigating Automotive Industry Headwinds: Impacts on Global Immigration Strategies.” Fragomen , Webinar, 4 February 2025.
Speaker, immigration related topics during conferences presented by the Council for Global Immigration and the American Immigration Lawyers Association.
Speaker, “Has The Mobility Industry Opened Pandora’s Suitcase? Part II - Business Travel Immigration Compliance.” Minnesota Employee Relocation Council , St. Paul, Minnesota, 13 July 2021.
Featured, “People on the Move.” Crain’s Chicago Business , 5 August 2019.
Featured, “Shearn takes reigns of Fragomen office in Chicago.” Chicago Daily Law Bulletin , 1 August 2019.
“Off the “Fast” Track? The Effect of Corporate Downsizing on Labor Certification Strategy and Procedure,” 4th Annual AILA New York Chapter Immigration Law Symposium Conference Handbook, 2001.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
